description:
Design a Concentrated Solar Power (CSP) pilot plant in Las Vegas by placing a 50-meter Solar Tower surrounded by at least 30 Heliostats, and analyze its yield on the Winter Solstice.

Instructions:
1. Draw a Foundation large enough to hold the solar field (e.g., 100m x 100m).
2. Place a Solar Tower in the center of the foundation and set its height to approximately 50 meters.
3. Place a field of at least 30 Heliostats (tracking mirrors) on the foundation, surrounding the tower. (You can place these manually or use the software's field-generation features).
4. Change the project location/city to Las Vegas, NV to simulate a high Direct Normal Irradiance (DNI) environment.
5. Set the simulation date to December 21 (Winter Solstice) to analyze the worst-case winter yield.
6. Run the Daily Yield Analysis (or Daily Environmental Temperature/Solar Radiation analysis) to calculate the concentrated energy arriving at the tower.
7. Save the project to exactly: ~/Documents/Energy3D/csp_las_vegas.ng3
